title: Butternut Squash & Goat Cheese Lasagne
description: Layers of pasta, velvety butternut purée, creamy goat cheese and a spinach-rosemary béchamel — a warming, elegant vegetarian centrepiece.
steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C (fan). Toss the diced butternut squash with the olive oil, salt and pepper on a baking tray and roast for 25 minutes until tender and lightly caramelised. Blend into a smooth purée and set aside.

Make the béchamel: melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat, whisk in the flour and cook for 1 minute. Gradually pour in the milk, whisking constantly, until you have a smooth, thick sauce. Season with salt, pepper and nutmeg, then stir in the rosemary. Remove from the heat and fold in the spinach — it will wilt into the sauce. Set aside.
In a dry pan over medium heat, briefly fry the sage leaves in a drop of olive oil for 30 seconds until crisp. Set aside.

Assemble the lasagne in a baking dish: start with a thin layer of béchamel, then a layer of pasta sheets, then half the butternut purée, half the goat cheese crumbled over, and a ladleful of béchamel. Repeat the layers, finishing with the remaining béchamel on top.
Scatter the grated parmesan over the top and arrange the crispy sage leaves over the surface.
Bake for 35-40 minutes until the top is golden and bubbling and the pasta is cooked through. Rest for 10 minutes before serving.
footnote: Roasting the squash rather than steaming it concentrates its sweetness and keeps the purée from being watery — essential for a lasagne that holds its shape when served.
